name: Web — Fetch & Search
summary: Fetch a URL or search the web. Always use command.run with curl or Python — never echo the URL as text.

commands.name: fetch_url
commands.description: Fetch and read a web page. Use for: 'what is on X', 'latest post on X', 'check X website'. Replace <url> with the actual URL.

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search the web for a query. Use for: 'search for X', 'find information about X', 'what is X'.
commands.example: {"plan": [{"action": "command.run", "params": {"cmd": "python3 -c \"import urllib.parse,urllib.request; q='latest Python release'; u='https://duckduckgo.com/html/?q='+urllib.parse.quote(q); print(urllib.request.urlopen(u,timeout=20).read(12000).decode('utf-8','ignore'))\""}, "description": "Web search"}], "message": "Searching the web"}

commands.name: fetch_json
commands.description: Fetch and pretty-print a JSON API endpoint.
